title: The South By Langston Hughes | Explanation In Hindi
description:
Few poets captured the bitter geography of racism as sharply as Langston Hughes did in "The South," where the land itself becomes both seducer and oppressor — beautiful, brutal, and soaked in the blood of those it claims to love. Written during the Harlem Renaissance, this poem tears open the contradiction of Black Americans being told to belong to a land that denies them humanity, making it essential reading for BA English Honours students across DU, SOL, and IGNOU. Hughes weaponizes irony so precisely here that every line feels like a wound dressed as a landscape.
